morning to the call OF the railroad
for men. as the average construc-
ton labOrer will not risk his life
IN working in landslides at the
measly wage OF $l.75 per day, when
ether roads are paying 1925 for
help on straight work in which
there is no danger.


There has been much criticism
heard today in this offer of the
Great Northern of $l75 for labor
ing men to aid in an emergency of
this kind, when the wives and chill
dren of wreck victims are clamor-
ing for news of the missing or the
return of their dead.


1t was at first reported that the
Great Northern wanted men at any
price for the work of digging out
the buried trains at wellington. but
the wage offered Spokane laborers
does not bear out this statement.
